title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Wins Oscar for Best Cinematography - IndieWire
text: Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw won the Academy Award for Best Cinematography for her work on Ryan Coogler's film Sinners, becoming the first woman ever to win in this category and the first woman of color to be nominated. During her acceptance speech, Arkapaw praised director Coogler's collaborative approach. She recalled: Whenever I say thank you to Ryan, he replies, No, thank you, highlighting his trust and belief in her talents. She then asked all women in the audience to stand, expressing gratitude for their support throughout awards season. Addressing the significance of her achievement, Arkapaw emphasized its broader impact: These moments happen because of you guys, she told the assembled industry professionals, before thanking the cast and crew for their beauty and professionalism. Arkapaw's cinematographic approach for Sinners was groundbreaking. The film alternated between Ultra Panavision 70 and 65mm IMAX, creating a unique visual experience. She incorporated anamorphic lenses, some custom-engineered by Panavision's Dan Sasaki, along with vintage glass from classic films like 2001: A Space Odyssey to achieve period authenticity with visceral immediacy. This marked Arkapaw's second collaboration with Coogler following Black Panther: Wakanda Forever.
